prompt: Eye-level wide view of a dated 1990s Salem NH primary bathroom BEFORE remodel. Builder-grade oak vanity with a single integrated cultured-marble sink top, brass-tone faucet, hollow particle-board doors. Tan four-inch ceramic floor tile with darker stained grout. A fiberglass tub-shower combo behind a sliding bypass glass door. A vinyl wallpaper border at chair-rail height in a tired floral print. A wall-mounted mirror with a Hollywood bulb-strip vanity light fixture above. Shot with a 35mm prime lens, harsh overhead fluorescent and the bare-bulb vanity strip combine to make warm-yellow but unflattering light. A red painter's drop cloth folded on the floor as the only saturated color. Slightly desaturated, NOT HGTV bright, working-contractor proof-of-work feel. No readable text, no logos.
